top_strategic.title: Propose new lab: content-virality-mechanics
top_strategic.evidence_summary:
Shot type, production quality, and engagement patterns form a cohesive domain spanning multiple labs. A dedicated lab can systematically map what makes pickleball content go viral and productize those insights for creators and platforms.

Evidence: pickle-idea-lab shot_scientist identifies overhead shots drive 5.12x viral lift; production_auditor achieves F1=0.99 on badge prediction; frequency_analyst shows 'strategic' dominates 'arc' taxonomy (84%); entity_profiler identifies 27% low-quality content archetype
top_strategic.recommended_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py content-virality-mechanics — then customize config. Suggested researchers: Video Virality Scientist, Content Production Analyst, Engagement Economist

proposal.rationale: Shot type, production quality, and engagement patterns form a cohesive domain spanning multiple labs. A dedicated lab can systematically map what makes pickleball content go viral and productize those insights for creators and platforms.
proposal.evidence: pickle-idea-lab shot_scientist identifies overhead shots drive 5.12x viral lift; production_auditor achieves F1=0.99 on badge prediction; frequency_analyst shows 'strategic' dominates 'arc' taxonomy (84%); entity_profiler identifies 27% low-quality content archetype
proposal.business_context: Package viral shot patterns and production playbooks as creator tools, platform recommendation features, and media training for player-influencers seeking sponsorship deals.

draft_config.description: Shot type, production quality, and engagement patterns form a cohesive domain spanning multiple labs. A dedicated lab can systematically map what makes pickleball content go viral and productize those insights for creators and platforms.
draft_config.business_context: Package viral shot patterns and production playbooks as creator tools, platform recommendation features, and media training for player-influencers seeking sponsorship deals.

top_strategic.plausibility_note: Social media APIs (Twitter, TikTok, YouTube) readily available and virality mechanics are largely statistical pattern-matching—most feasible with off-shelf tools.

multi_model_check.change_mind: Evidence is circumstantial—shows *correlation* across labs but not *causal integration*. Would need: (a) demonstration that existing labs actively *cannot* answer virality questions, (b) quantified gaps in current coverage, (c) pilot results showing the dedicated lab finds non-redundant insights, (d) scope boundaries (virality of what? short-form only? all platforms?).
multi_model_check.blind_spot: No evidence that fragmentation across labs is actually a *problem* vs. healthy specialization. May create siloed virality research that duplicates effort. Missing: cost-benefit analysis of consolidation vs. cross-lab connectors. Also assumes 'cohesive domain' without defining domain boundaries or success metrics for the new lab.

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'Propose new lab: content-virality-mechanics'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py content-virality-mechanics — then customize config. Suggested researchers: Video Virality Scientist, Content Production Analyst, Engagement Economist

top_strategic.title: Propose new lab: player-skill-quantification
top_strategic.evidence_summary:
Player skill assessment appears across multiple labs (skill level gaps, consistency-composure correlation, longitudinal tracking) but lacks a unified measurement framework. This deserves dedicated research into skill taxonomies, rating systems, and validation methodologies.

Evidence: general-purpose gap_finder identifies 'Player skill level / rating classification' gap; correlation_scout finds strong consistency↔composure link (r=0.90); pickle-idea-lab opportunity_scout flags 65 players with 5+ clips enabling longitudinal skill tracking
top_strategic.recommended_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py player-skill-quantification — then customize config. Suggested researchers: Sports Psychometrician, Rating Systems Designer, Longitudinal Data Analyst

proposal.rationale: Player skill assessment appears across multiple labs (skill level gaps, consistency-composure correlation, longitudinal tracking) but lacks a unified measurement framework. This deserves dedicated research into skill taxonomies, rating systems, and validation methodologies.
proposal.evidence: general-purpose gap_finder identifies 'Player skill level / rating classification' gap; correlation_scout finds strong consistency↔composure link (r=0.90); pickle-idea-lab opportunity_scout flags 65 players with 5+ clips enabling longitudinal skill tracking
proposal.business_context: Build defensible skill tiers and player progression models that unlock personalized coaching products, tournament seeding automation, and player-brand matching for sponsorship deals.

draft_config.description: Player skill assessment appears across multiple labs (skill level gaps, consistency-composure correlation, longitudinal tracking) but lacks a unified measurement framework. This deserves dedicated research into skill taxonomies, rating systems, and validation methodologies.
draft_config.business_context: Build defensible skill tiers and player progression models that unlock personalized coaching products, tournament seeding automation, and player-brand matching for sponsorship deals.

top_strategic.plausibility_note: Player data APIs exist (Steam, game publishers) but skill quantification requires complex ML modeling and validation against ground truth—doable but tight timeline.

multi_model_check.change_mind: Stronger case than Finding 1—skill assessment *is* clearly multi-lab. But evidence stops at observing the pattern. Would need: (a) explicit gaps in current quantification methods, (b) failure modes from fragmentation (conflicting skill scores?), (c) prototype unified framework and validation against ground truth, (d) stakeholder feedback that fragmentation causes decisions-making problems.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: Assumes unification is desirable—may lose domain-specific nuance (e.g., skill in basketball ≠ skill in soccer). No evidence that players/scouts *want* a single skill score vs. multi-dimensional profiles. Missing: whether the 'lack of unification' is actually blocking downstream decisions.

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'Propose new lab: player-skill-quantification'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py player-skill-quantification — then customize config. Suggested researchers: Sports Psychometrician, Rating Systems Designer, Longitudinal Data Analyst

top_strategic.title: Propose new lab: sponsorship-intelligence-engine
top_strategic.evidence_summary:
Multiple opportunity_scout findings converge on sponsorship whitespace, brand co-occurrence patterns, and player-brand affinities—but lack centralized analysis of deal mechanics, category saturation, and ROI prediction.

Evidence: pickle-idea-lab opportunity_scout identifies 4 equipment categories with ZERO brand detection; 4185 clips (97%) with sponsorship whitespace; 179 player-brand pairs with 3+ co-occurrences; social caption generation ready at 99.9%
top_strategic.recommended_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py sponsorship-intelligence-engine — then customize config. Suggested researchers: Sports Marketing Economist, Brand Intelligence Analyst, Influencer Valuation Specialist

proposal.rationale: Multiple opportunity_scout findings converge on sponsorship whitespace, brand co-occurrence patterns, and player-brand affinities—but lack centralized analysis of deal mechanics, category saturation, and ROI prediction.
proposal.evidence: pickle-idea-lab opportunity_scout identifies 4 equipment categories with ZERO brand detection; 4185 clips (97%) with sponsorship whitespace; 179 player-brand pairs with 3+ co-occurrences; social caption generation ready at 99.9%
proposal.business_context: Enable B2B sponsorship marketplace matching—brands find undermonetized players/content; players find high-fit sponsors; platform takes commission on deal velocity and contract optimization.

draft_config.description: Multiple opportunity_scout findings converge on sponsorship whitespace, brand co-occurrence patterns, and player-brand affinities—but lack centralized analysis of deal mechanics, category saturation, and ROI prediction.
draft_config.business_context: Enable B2B sponsorship marketplace matching—brands find undermonetized players/content; players find high-fit sponsors; platform takes commission on deal velocity and contract optimization.

top_strategic.plausibility_note: Sponsorship data is fragmented, proprietary, and requires manual enrichment; regulatory compliance for financial/endorsement disclosures adds friction.

multi_model_check.change_mind: Lowest confidence score justified. Evidence is weakest here: 'convergence' and 'whitespace' are vague. Would need: (a) concrete sponsorship opportunities *missed* by current labs, (b) financial ROI model for a dedicated engine, (c) sample of brand-player matches the current system fails to detect, (d) competitive analysis (do existing tools/agencies cover this?), (e) domain expert buy-in.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: Assumes sponsorship matching is a *research* problem, not a *business operations* problem (likely handled by agents/managers already). No evidence this is AI-research territory. Missing: Why labs aren't surfacing this now—is it deprioritization or genuine blindness? Risk of building a tool nobody needs.

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'Propose new lab: sponsorship-intelligence-engine'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py sponsorship-intelligence-engine — then customize config. Suggested researchers: Sports Marketing Economist, Brand Intelligence Analyst, Influencer Valuation Specialist

top_strategic.title: No clear persona gaps detected across 2 labs
top_strategic.evidence_summary: No heavy category concentration or recurring DA themes warrant new personas right now. Will re-check as more runs accumulate.
top_strategic.recommended_action: Continue daily runs — patterns will emerge.
top_strategic.plausibility_note: No clear direction means wasted time on problem discovery; 30 days with undefined scope strongly favors failure.

multi_model_check.change_mind: Null finding is well-justified and appropriately low-confidence (0.008). However, would become 'partial' if: (a) you identify *why* no gaps exist (is coverage genuinely complete, or just not yet analyzed?), (b) you define what *would* trigger a new persona (threshold rules), (c) you show confidence grows with *volume* of runs analyzed (2 labs may be insufficient).
multi_model_check.blind_spot: No distinction between 'no gaps found' and 'gaps not yet visible.' Missing: metadata on run diversity—are the 2 labs exploring overlapping audience segments? A baseline assumption of coverage completeness is risky without sampling rigor.

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'No clear persona gaps detected across 2 labs'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Continue daily runs — patterns will emerge.

top_data.title: Category 'wildcard_idea' active in 2 labs
top_data.evidence_summary: The finding category 'wildcard_idea' is being produced by multiple labs: {'general-purpose': 7, 'pickle-idea-lab': 8}. This suggests a cross-cutting pattern type.
top_data.recommended_action: Review findings in this category across labs for deeper synthesis.

multi_model_check.change_mind: Observation is valid (15 instances across 2 labs suggests signal), but interpretation is under-specified. Would need: (a) definition of 'cross-cutting pattern'—is 15 events statistically meaningful given total output volume? (b) qualitative analysis: are these the *same* wildcard ideas or coincidental category label reuse? (c) evidence that labs should coordinate on these or stay independent, (d) comparison to baseline category co-occurrence rates.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: Conflates *frequency* with *importance*. A category appearing in multiple labs could indicate: (i) genuine cross-cutting insight, (ii) vague labeling by agents, or (iii) unrelated ideas bundled by label. Missing: semantic clustering to check if these wildcard ideas are actually about the same thing. Also: no mechanism proposed—does observation trigger action?

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'Category 'wildcard_idea' active in 2 labs'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Review findings in this category across labs for deeper synthesis.

top_data.title: No lab subscriptions configured
top_data.evidence_summary: No labs have declared subscriptions yet. Add `subscriptions: ["lab-id:researcher"]` to a lab's config to enable cross-lab signal routing.
top_data.recommended_action: Edit lab_config.json files to declare subscriptions between labs.

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'No lab subscriptions configured'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Edit lab_config.json files to declare subscriptions between labs.

Would need: (a) concrete sponsorship opportunities *missed* by current labs, (b) financial ROI model for a dedicated engine, (c) sample of brand-player matches the current system fails to detect, (d) competitive analysis (do existing tools/agencies cover this?), (e) domain expert buy-in.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: Assumes sponsorship matching is a *research* problem, not a *business operations* problem (likely handled by agents/managers already). No evidence this is AI-research territory. Missing: Why labs aren't surfacing this now—is it deprioritization or genuine blindness? Risk of building a tool nobody needs.

all_findings.title: Propose new lab: content-virality-mechanics
all_findings.evidence_summary:
Shot type, production quality, and engagement patterns form a cohesive domain spanning multiple labs. A dedicated lab can systematically map what makes pickleball content go viral and productize those insights for creators and platforms.

Evidence: pickle-idea-lab shot_scientist identifies overhead shots drive 5.12x viral lift; production_auditor achieves F1=0.99 on badge prediction; frequency_analyst shows 'strategic' dominates 'arc' taxonomy (84%); entity_profiler identifies 27% low-quality content archetype
all_findings.recommended_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py content-virality-mechanics — then customize config. Suggested researchers: Video Virality Scientist, Content Production Analyst, Engagement Economist

proposal.rationale: Shot type, production quality, and engagement patterns form a cohesive domain spanning multiple labs. A dedicated lab can systematically map what makes pickleball content go viral and productize those insights for creators and platforms.
proposal.evidence: pickle-idea-lab shot_scientist identifies overhead shots drive 5.12x viral lift; production_auditor achieves F1=0.99 on badge prediction; frequency_analyst shows 'strategic' dominates 'arc' taxonomy (84%); entity_profiler identifies 27% low-quality content archetype
proposal.business_context: Package viral shot patterns and production playbooks as creator tools, platform recommendation features, and media training for player-influencers seeking sponsorship deals.

draft_config.description: Shot type, production quality, and engagement patterns form a cohesive domain spanning multiple labs. A dedicated lab can systematically map what makes pickleball content go viral and productize those insights for creators and platforms.
draft_config.business_context: Package viral shot patterns and production playbooks as creator tools, platform recommendation features, and media training for player-influencers seeking sponsorship deals.

all_findings.plausibility_note: Social media APIs (Twitter, TikTok, YouTube) readily available and virality mechanics are largely statistical pattern-matching—most feasible with off-shelf tools.

multi_model_check.change_mind: Evidence is circumstantial—shows *correlation* across labs but not *causal integration*. Would need: (a) demonstration that existing labs actively *cannot* answer virality questions, (b) quantified gaps in current coverage, (c) pilot results showing the dedicated lab finds non-redundant insights, (d) scope boundaries (virality of what? short-form only? all platforms?).
multi_model_check.blind_spot: No evidence that fragmentation across labs is actually a *problem* vs. healthy specialization. May create siloed virality research that duplicates effort. Missing: cost-benefit analysis of consolidation vs. cross-lab connectors. Also assumes 'cohesive domain' without defining domain boundaries or success metrics for the new lab.

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'Propose new lab: content-virality-mechanics'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py content-virality-mechanics — then customize config. Suggested researchers: Video Virality Scientist, Content Production Analyst, Engagement Economist

all_findings.title: Propose new lab: player-skill-quantification
all_findings.evidence_summary:
Player skill assessment appears across multiple labs (skill level gaps, consistency-composure correlation, longitudinal tracking) but lacks a unified measurement framework. This deserves dedicated research into skill taxonomies, rating systems, and validation methodologies.

Evidence: general-purpose gap_finder identifies 'Player skill level / rating classification' gap; correlation_scout finds strong consistency↔composure link (r=0.90); pickle-idea-lab opportunity_scout flags 65 players with 5+ clips enabling longitudinal skill tracking
all_findings.recommended_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py player-skill-quantification — then customize config. Suggested researchers: Sports Psychometrician, Rating Systems Designer, Longitudinal Data Analyst

proposal.rationale: Player skill assessment appears across multiple labs (skill level gaps, consistency-composure correlation, longitudinal tracking) but lacks a unified measurement framework. This deserves dedicated research into skill taxonomies, rating systems, and validation methodologies.
proposal.evidence: general-purpose gap_finder identifies 'Player skill level / rating classification' gap; correlation_scout finds strong consistency↔composure link (r=0.90); pickle-idea-lab opportunity_scout flags 65 players with 5+ clips enabling longitudinal skill tracking
proposal.business_context: Build defensible skill tiers and player progression models that unlock personalized coaching products, tournament seeding automation, and player-brand matching for sponsorship deals.

draft_config.description: Player skill assessment appears across multiple labs (skill level gaps, consistency-composure correlation, longitudinal tracking) but lacks a unified measurement framework. This deserves dedicated research into skill taxonomies, rating systems, and validation methodologies.
draft_config.business_context: Build defensible skill tiers and player progression models that unlock personalized coaching products, tournament seeding automation, and player-brand matching for sponsorship deals.

all_findings.plausibility_note: Player data APIs exist (Steam, game publishers) but skill quantification requires complex ML modeling and validation against ground truth—doable but tight timeline.

multi_model_check.change_mind: Stronger case than Finding 1—skill assessment *is* clearly multi-lab. But evidence stops at observing the pattern. Would need: (a) explicit gaps in current quantification methods, (b) failure modes from fragmentation (conflicting skill scores?), (c) prototype unified framework and validation against ground truth, (d) stakeholder feedback that fragmentation causes decisions-making problems.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: Assumes unification is desirable—may lose domain-specific nuance (e.g., skill in basketball ≠ skill in soccer). No evidence that players/scouts *want* a single skill score vs. multi-dimensional profiles. Missing: whether the 'lack of unification' is actually blocking downstream decisions.

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'Propose new lab: player-skill-quantification'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py player-skill-quantification — then customize config. Suggested researchers: Sports Psychometrician, Rating Systems Designer, Longitudinal Data Analyst

all_findings.title: Category 'wildcard_idea' active in 2 labs
all_findings.evidence_summary: The finding category 'wildcard_idea' is being produced by multiple labs: {'general-purpose': 7, 'pickle-idea-lab': 8}. This suggests a cross-cutting pattern type.
all_findings.recommended_action: Review findings in this category across labs for deeper synthesis.

multi_model_check.change_mind: Observation is valid (15 instances across 2 labs suggests signal), but interpretation is under-specified. Would need: (a) definition of 'cross-cutting pattern'—is 15 events statistically meaningful given total output volume? (b) qualitative analysis: are these the *same* wildcard ideas or coincidental category label reuse? (c) evidence that labs should coordinate on these or stay independent, (d) comparison to baseline category co-occurrence rates.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: Conflates *frequency* with *importance*. A category appearing in multiple labs could indicate: (i) genuine cross-cutting insight, (ii) vague labeling by agents, or (iii) unrelated ideas bundled by label. Missing: semantic clustering to check if these wildcard ideas are actually about the same thing. Also: no mechanism proposed—does observation trigger action?

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'Category 'wildcard_idea' active in 2 labs'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Review findings in this category across labs for deeper synthesis.

all_findings.title: Propose new lab: sponsorship-intelligence-engine
all_findings.evidence_summary:
Multiple opportunity_scout findings converge on sponsorship whitespace, brand co-occurrence patterns, and player-brand affinities—but lack centralized analysis of deal mechanics, category saturation, and ROI prediction.

Evidence: pickle-idea-lab opportunity_scout identifies 4 equipment categories with ZERO brand detection; 4185 clips (97%) with sponsorship whitespace; 179 player-brand pairs with 3+ co-occurrences; social caption generation ready at 99.9%
all_findings.recommended_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py sponsorship-intelligence-engine — then customize config. Suggested researchers: Sports Marketing Economist, Brand Intelligence Analyst, Influencer Valuation Specialist

proposal.rationale: Multiple opportunity_scout findings converge on sponsorship whitespace, brand co-occurrence patterns, and player-brand affinities—but lack centralized analysis of deal mechanics, category saturation, and ROI prediction.
proposal.evidence: pickle-idea-lab opportunity_scout identifies 4 equipment categories with ZERO brand detection; 4185 clips (97%) with sponsorship whitespace; 179 player-brand pairs with 3+ co-occurrences; social caption generation ready at 99.9%
proposal.business_context: Enable B2B sponsorship marketplace matching—brands find undermonetized players/content; players find high-fit sponsors; platform takes commission on deal velocity and contract optimization.

multi_model_check.blind_spot: Assumes sponsorship matching is a *research* problem, not a *business operations* problem (likely handled by agents/managers already). No evidence this is AI-research territory. Missing: Why labs aren't surfacing this now—is it deprioritization or genuine blindness? Risk of building a tool nobody needs.
